description:
Add `@type name :: <type-expr>` declarations and a module-level ModuleTypeEnv mapping name → Descr.

## Syntax

```
defmodule Foo do
  @type id :: integer
  @type pair :: {id, id}
  @type list_of_id :: [id]

  @spec lookup(id) :: id | nil
  fn lookup(x) do ... end
end
```

## Mechanics

1. Parser: `@type Name :: <type-expr>` becomes Attribute::TypeAlias(TypeAliasDecl { name, body_tokens }) attached to ModuleDef.attrs (.31.2 enables this).
2. Pre-typer pass: collect all @type attrs from a ModuleDef into ModuleTypeEnv: HashMap<String, Descr>. Resolve named refs via .31.1's parser, threading the env through for lookup.
3. Cycle detection: a → b → a should error with a clean diagnostic (use .20.5 DiagCode taxonomy).
4. Forward references allowed within a module: collect all names first, then resolve bodies (two-phase).

## Where it lives

- src/ir_lower.rs or a new src/type_env.rs: builds ModuleTypeEnv from ModuleDef.attrs before ir_typer runs
- The env flows into the typer? Or only into validation (.31.5)? Decision: only into validation. The typer infers from real Descrs; @type aliases are user-facing names, not part of inference. ModuleTypeEnv is consulted only when parsing user-written @spec bodies and @type bodies.

## Tests

- Simple alias: @type id :: integer; @spec foo(id) :: id parses and resolves
- Alias-of-alias: @type a :: integer; @type b :: a; resolves transitively
- Cycle: @type a :: b; @type b :: a; errors with cycle diagnostic
- Undefined: @spec foo(undefined_name) :: integer errors with unknown-type diagnostic
- Shadowing: @type integer :: float would shadow a builtin — pick a rule (probably reject) and test it

## Out of scope

- Parameterized aliases (@type result(t) :: ...) — deferred
- Cross-module aliases (Mod.SomeType) — deferred
title: @type aliases + module type environment
